description_long: This object is a vintage, upright grinder with a robust metallic body, featuring a cylindrical main shaft and a sturdy green base. The central column is composed of brass or bronze-colored metal, with a prominent focusing knob and a side-mounted circular magnifying lens. The grinding platform is flat and black, supported by two vertical posts, and includes adjustment knobs for precise operation. The base is rectangular and painted green, providing stability and a classic industrial appearance. The object has several exposed mechanical components, including adjustment screws and a movable stage. The overall design suggests durability and precision, with a utilitarian aesthetic typical of early 20th-century workshop tools.
description: A vintage, upright metal grinder with a cylindrical shaft, green base, and side-mounted magnifying lens. It features adjustment knobs, a flat grinding platform, and exposed mechanical components.
description_view_0: Front view shows the cylindrical shaft, magnifying lens, adjustment knobs, and the flat grinding platform clearly.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the angled shaft, the depth of the grinding platform, and the supporting posts.
description_view_2: Top-down view emphasizes the circular shaft, magnifying lens, and the rectangular green base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals the angled shaft, adjustment knobs, and the mechanical structure supporting the platform.
attribute_handle_design: The handle is a small, round adjustment knob located on the side of the shaft, designed for precise manual control. It lacks ergonomic texturing but is easily accessible for fine adjustments.
